content.full_text:
October 21, 1977


Robert E. Shaw
Mobil Oil Corporation
925 "L" Street, Suite 850
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Shaw:

     This letter is in response to our telephone conversation on
October 3, 1977 regarding retention of records by a lobbyist.

     Lobbyists are required to support their expenditure journal
with original source documents, such as receipts, invoices and
paid checks (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18615(a)(1)(B)(2)).  The
retention by a lobbyist of a complete photocopy of all original
source documents will meet the requirements of this regulation
and will be acceptable as documentation for audit purposes pro-
vided that the original documents are maintained and can be made
available upon request.

     If I can be of further assistance, do not hesitate to
contact me.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Alan Herndon
                                        Compliance Representative

AH:bw

sections.conclusion: Lobbyists are required to support their expenditure journal with original source documents, such as receipts, invoices and paid checks (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18501.5(a)(1)(B)(2)). The retention by a lobbyist of a complete photocopy of all original source documents will meet the requirements of this regulation and will be acceptable as documentation for audit purposes provided that the original documents are maintained and can be made available upon request.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether lobbyists may retain photocopies of original source documents (receipts, invoices, paid checks) to support their expenditure journals, or if original documents must be retained.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC concludes that lobbyists may retain photocopies of original source documents to satisfy record-keeping requirements, provided that the original documents are also maintained and made available upon request for audit purposes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal letter responding to telephone conversation; no formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections marked, but clear question and answer structure present. Minor OCR quality issues (e.g., 'conversntion' for 'conversation', 'snaw' for 'Shaw') but content is clear. Regulation citation provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether lobbyists may retain photocopies of original source documents (receipts, invoices, paid checks) to support their expenditure journals, or if original documents must be retained.

CONCLUSION: Lobbyists are required to support their expenditure journal with original source documents, such as receipts, invoices and paid checks (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18501.5(a)(1)(B)(2)). The retention by a lobbyist of a complete photocopy of all original source documents will meet the requirements of this regulation and will be acceptable as documentation for audit purposes provided that the original documents are maintained and can be made available upon request.

Shaw Mobil Oil Corporation 925 "L" Street, Suite 850 Sacramento, California 95814 Dear Mr. Shaw: This letter is in response to our telephone conversation on October 3, 1977 regarding retention of records by a lobbyist. Lobbyists are required to support their expenditure journal with original source documents, such as receipts, invoices and paid checks (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18615(a)(1)(B)(2)). The retention by a lobbyist of a complete photocopy of all original source documents will meet the requirements of this regulation and will be acceptable as documentation for audit purposes pro- vided that the original documents are maintained and can be made available upon request. If I can be of further assistance, do not hesitate to contact me. Sincerely, Alan Herndon Compliance Representative AH:bw
embedding.summary: FPPC advises that lobbyists may retain complete photocopies of original source documents (receipts, invoices, checks) supporting their expenditure journals as documentation for audit purposes, as long as the original documents are also preserved and available upon request.
